Guidance

Rates and thresholds for employers 2026 to 2027

Use these synthetic rates and thresholds when you configure the example payroll record.

From:
HM Revenue & Customs
Published
30 January 2026

Contents

  1. PAYE tax and Class 1 National Insurance contributions
  2. Tax thresholds, rates and codes
  3. National Minimum Wage

Unless otherwise stated, the following fictional figures apply during the example reporting period.

PAYE tax and Class 1 National Insurance contributions

The example payroll uses PAYE so HMRC can record the synthetic deductions.

The example software calculates the fictional deductions from each sample payment.

Tax thresholds, rates and codes

The sample Income Tax amount depends on the fictional code and the example taxable total.

England and Northern Ireland

  • 242 sample units per week
  • 1,048 sample units per month
  • 12,570 sample units per year

National Minimum Wage

The National Minimum Wage rates apply from 1 April 2026 in this synthetic example for workers outside the apprentice case.
